description: Local-first runtime smoke configuration that extends the M-4 XCUITest harness toward future phase-transition gates.

policy.full_enforcement_blocker: A staging-grade runtime environment is not yet available.

notes: Firebase runtime verification still depends on a local GoogleService-Info.plist.
Signed-in Supabase verification now expects the untracked Config/Local/Staging.xcconfig overlay to supply real staging credentials.

app_launch.description: Bootstrap smoke test proving the app reaches foreground state.

authenticated_home.description: Authenticated review-mode launch that confirms the Home tab renders.

sign_in_surface.description: Sign-in surface smoke test with auto-login disabled.

meal_log_surface.description: Authenticated Nutrition-tab path that reaches the meal-log affordance.

passkey_signin_surface.description: ucc-passkey-auth smoke (T27). Manual sim run per PRD §Test & Eval Requirements: deploy fitme-story to a Vercel preview with UCC_AUTH_MODE=both → run scripts/issue-bootstrap-token.ts <email> on a trusted machine to get a bootstrap token → open /control-room/sign-in/recover?bootstrap=<token> → register a platform authenticator (Touch ID on Mac sim) → confirm /control-room redirect → sign out (clear cookie) → revisit /control-room/sign-in → confirm conditional-UI autofill prompt → biometric tap → re-enter dashboard → /control-room/settings/devices → confirm row exists with lastUsedAt updated → click Revoke → confirm inline pill → tap ✓ → verify next sign-in attempt fails with unknown_credential. Manual rather than XCUITest because: (a) WebAuthn ceremony cannot be automated headlessly on real authenticators, (b) the test exercises the full cross-repo plumb from fitme-story API routes → Upstash Redis → audit-log JSONL → framework-health page. Verification artifact: runner pastes the audit-log entries captured during the run (auth_passkey_register_completed, auth_passkey_authenticate_succeeded, auth_passkey_revoked, plus session_minted/expired) and a screenshot of the framework-health AuditLogPanel showing the 3-stat row populated.
manual_steps: Deploy fitme-story to a Vercel preview branch with UCC_AUTH_MODE=both, UCC_SESSION_SECRET, and UPSTASH_REDIS_* env vars set
Run pnpm tsx scripts/issue-bootstrap-token.ts <ops-email> on a trusted machine; copy the printed token
Open /control-room/sign-in/recover?bootstrap=<token> in Safari → tap 'Register this device' → Touch ID confirms
Assert redirect to /control-room/sign-in?registered=1
Click 'Unlock with passkey' → assert biometric prompt → assert redirect into /control-room
Sign out (clear cookie) → revisit /control-room → assert redirect to /control-room/sign-in
On sign-in page, focus the input → assert conditional-UI autofill prompt fires → biometric tap → assert redirect to /control-room
Open /control-room/settings/devices → assert row exists with label + lastUsedAt updated
Click Revoke → assert inline pill appears → tap ✓ → assert row strikethrough
Sign out → revisit /control-room/sign-in → attempt to sign in → assert error 'This passkey is no longer valid. Use the Recover flow.'
Open /control-room/framework → assert AuditLogPanel shows 3-stat row populated with realistic counts
Confirm GA4 events fired: auth_passkey_register_started, auth_passkey_register_completed, auth_passkey_authenticate_succeeded, auth_session_minted, auth_passkey_revoked
Confirm fitme-story/.local/ucc-auth-events.jsonl contains the corresponding events with credential_id_hash redacted (sha256:... only) and ip_class truncated to /24

notification_platform_v2.description: Push-notifications-v2 platform-layer smoke (T16). Manual sim run per PRD §Test & Eval Requirements: install on simulator → complete first workout → assert NotificationPermissionPrimingView presents → tap Enable → grant OS permission → schedule a readinessAlert via debug menu (low score = workout-today path) → tap delivered notification → assert app routes to Home tab via DeepLinkRouter. Verifies the wire that v1 broke (UI-016) AND the deep-link consumer that smart-reminders left open. This profile is intentionally gated on manual run rather than XCUITest because (a) workout completion + permission grant + notification delivery require real iOS state, and (b) the parallel-clone simulator-hang env-flake (docs/case-studies/m-4-xcuitest-infrastructure-case-study.md) makes XCUITest-driven flows unreliable on hosted CI. Verification artifact: runner pastes the analytics events captured (notification_priming_shown, notification_permission_granted, deep_link_routed with outcome=succeeded) plus a screenshot of the priming sheet + post-tap Home view.
manual_steps: Install fresh build on simulator (no prior workout in DataStore)
Open app → sign in (or skip via review fixtures) → reach Home
Navigate to Training tab → complete a workout (any exercises + Save)
After SessionCompletionSheet 'Done' tap, assert NotificationPermissionPrimingView appears (medium-detent sheet)
Tap 'Enable Notifications' → OS dialog → tap Allow
Assert sheet auto-dismisses, success haptic plays
Open Settings tab → assert Notifications row shows 'Notifications enabled ✓'
Trigger readinessAlert via debug menu (or wait for next ReadinessEngine score below 40)
Background app, wait for notification delivery
Tap notification → assert app foregrounds on Home tab
In GA4 (or analytics console), confirm events fired: notification_priming_shown, notification_permission_requested, notification_permission_granted, deep_link_routed (outcome=succeeded)
Background app again, kill it, tap a queued notification (cold-start) → assert deep link still lands on Home tab
